result.summary: Mapped BigQuery’s public developer surface on bigquery.googleapis.com: 1 REST API, 1 public MCP server endpoint, and the bq CLI; REST/CLI use Google Cloud OAuth/ADC, while the MCP endpoint is seed-confirmed unauthenticated.

gcp_oauth_token.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token / Application Default Credentials
gcp_oauth_token.setup: Authenticate to Google Cloud for BigQuery using the easiest path described in [Authenticate to BigQuery](https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/authentication). For local development, run [`gcloud auth application-default login`](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/application-default/login) to create Application Default Credentials (ADC). For CLI use, you can also sign in with [`gcloud auth login`](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/login) or authorize a service account with [`gcloud auth activate-service-account`](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/activate-service-account). BigQuery REST methods require an OAuth access token with scopes such as `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/bigquery` or `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform` as shown in the API reference/discovery document.

variables.name: projectId
variables.description: Most methods are scoped to a Google Cloud project ID in the URL path.

packages.runtimeHint: install the Google Cloud CLI / SDK
